questions.text: A tangent to a circle is perpendicular to:
options: The radius at point of contact
questions.correctAnswer: The radius at point of contact
questions.explanation: The tangent at any point of a circle is perpendicular to the radius through the point of contact.

questions.text: From an external point, two tangents to a circle are equal.
questions.explanation: The lengths of tangents drawn from an external point to a circle are equal.

questions.text: How many tangents can be drawn from an external point to a circle?
questions.explanation: From any external point, exactly two tangents can be drawn to a circle. These tangents are equal in length.

questions.text: A line intersecting a circle in two points is called a ________.
questions.explanation: A secant is a line that intersects a circle at two distinct points. A tangent touches at only one point.
